options.name: --connection
options.description:
A connection string (can be PostgreSQL or
SQLServer or MySQL). For integrated auth, you
can use --database and --server instead.

options.name: --database
options.description:
Generates an integrated auth connection string
for the specified database.

options.name: --server
options.description:
Generates an integrated auth connection string
with the specified server (default: localhost).

options.name: --folder
options.description:
The folder containing your .sql migration files
(defaults to current working directory).

options.name: --preview
options.description: Run outstanding migrations, but roll them back.

options.name: --provider
options.description:
Use a specific database provider options:
sqlserver (default), postgresql, mysql.

options.name: --global
options.description:
Run all outstanding migrations in a single
transaction, if possible.

options.name: --table
options.description:
Name of the table used to track migrations
(default: Migrations).

options.name: --schema
options.description:
Name of the schema to be used to track
migrations (default: dbo for sqlserver, public
for postgresql, mysql for mysql).

options.name: --count
options.description: Print the number of outstanding migrations.
